description: A surface is 'helu' when it is even, with few bumps or ridges to catch touch or motion. The adjective can also describe hair or movement that proceeds without jolts. The word 'luwae' (graceful) adds an aesthetic judgement; 'helu' reports physical continuity.
articulatory_notes: Open breath through the glottis carries 'h' into true-mid front 'e'. The tongue tip then contacts the alveolar ridge for 'l', and air escapes along its sides into rounded high-back 'u'. The first syllable carries the word's stress.
sound_symbolism: Breath passes freely through 'h' and then continues around the sides of the tongue at 'l'. That uninterrupted route can help a speaker remember an even surface.
usage_notes: The phrase 'helu kerou' means smooth stone. Applied to motion, 'helu' reports passage without a catch or jolt.

semantic_domains.physical: an even surface or passage with little catching against touch or motion
